metadata.title: Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone

beats.narration_text: Bonjour. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of braess's paradox: the road that slows everyone. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of braess's paradox: the road that slows everyone
output: ✓ Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone

beats.narration_text: Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Adding a FREE road increases everyone's commute. Total cars: 100. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: splitSlider. Verify: Adding a FREE road increases everyone's commute. Total cars: 100. Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: splitSlider. Verify: Add

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.code_snippet: // Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone — core physics
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from yt-braess-paradox.html

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Show the core physics loop from yt-braess-paradox.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: braess's paradox: the road that slows everyone. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main slider from 0 to 100. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0 to 100

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main slider from 0 to 100

beats.narration_text: Adding a FREE road increases everyone's commute. Total cars: 100. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of braess's paradox: the road that slows everyone. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Braess's Paradox: The Road That Slows Everyone simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
